## {background-color="#533860"}

::: {style="color: white; font-size: 1.8em; font-weight: bold;"}
🎲 Graduate School: Large Networks
:::
::: {style="color: #FFE42A; font-size: 1.1em;"}
Probability & asymptotic methods for massive systems
:::

---

## Reality: Not So Easy {#grad-school-reality}

::: {.incremental}
- Need to iterate through **lots** of scenarios
- Large population → slow simulations
- Too slow to be practical
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Solution:** Assume population size is infinite
:::

---

## Asymptotic Approximations {#asymptotic-approx}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![Fluid dynamics](images/fluiddynamics.png)

::: {style="font-size: 0.75em; color: #555;"}
Fluid: continuous limit of discrete molecules
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![Ripples](images/Ripples.png)

::: {style="font-size: 0.75em; color: #555;"}
Waves: aggregate behavior, not individual particles
:::
:::
::::

---

## Wright-Fisher Diffusion {#wf-diffusion}

- **Asymptotic approximation** of the Wright-Fisher Model
- Continuous limit → independent of population size $N$
- One model works for any population

::: {.fragment}
**My research:**

- Completed: ODE approximation (much faster)
- In progress: *Accelerated* simulations via Large-Deviation Theory
:::

---

## {background-color="#533860"}

::: {style="color: white; font-size: 1.5em; font-weight: bold;"}
Load Balancing
:::
::: {style="color: #FFE42A; font-size: 1.1em;"}
Routing tasks in a large server network
:::

---

## Load Balancing {#load-balancing}

**Key question:** What's the best way to route tasks in a large server network?

![](images/LoadBalancing/Slide1.jpeg){fig-align="center" .r-stretch}

---

## Random Routing {#random-routing}

![](images/LoadBalancing/Slide2.jpeg){fig-align="center" .r-stretch}

::: {style="font-size: 0.8em;"}
Assign each task to a random server — simple, but inefficient
:::

---

## Power of Two Choices {#power-of-two}

![](images/LoadBalancing/Slide3.jpeg){fig-align="center" .r-stretch}

::: {style="font-size: 0.8em;"}
Sample 2 servers, pick the shorter queue
:::

---

## Power of Two: Dramatic Improvement {#power-of-two-2}

![](images/LoadBalancing/Slide4.jpeg){fig-align="center" .r-stretch}

---

## Join-the-Shortest-Queue {#jsq}

![](images/LoadBalancing/Slide5.jpeg){fig-align="center" .r-stretch}

::: {style="font-size: 0.8em;"}
Always join the shortest queue — optimal, but expensive to implement
:::

---

## Steady-State Distributions {#lb-steady-state}

![](images/LoadBalancing/decay.png){fig-align="center" .r-stretch}

::: {style="font-size: 0.8em;"}
JSQ achieves **exponential decay** in queue lengths — servers nearly always free
:::

---

## {background-color="#533860"}

::: {style="color: white; font-size: 1.5em; font-weight: bold;"}
File Caching: gLRU
:::
::: {style="color: #FFE42A; font-size: 1.1em;"}
Storing files efficiently in a large network
:::

---

## The File Caching Problem {#file-caching}

**Key question:** What's the best way to store files in a large network?

::: {.incremental}
- Standard **LRU** (Least-Recently-Used): evict oldest file
- Problem: files have **different sizes**
- Large files waste cache space
- Small files get unfairly evicted
:::

---

## gLRU: Chunk-Based Caching {#glru}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Standard LRU**

- Files treated as atomic units
- One big file crowds out many small ones
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**gLRU**

- Subdivide files into **equal-sized chunks**
- Add chunks of requested file
- Remove chunks of least-recently-used file
:::
::::

::: {.fragment}
→ Cache space used far more **uniformly**
:::

---

## gLRU: Key Results {#glru-result}

::: {.incremental}
- **~32% reduction** in video streaming stall duration vs. standard LRU
- Validated on real-world data (MSR Cambridge Traces)
- Published in **ACM TOMPECS** (2019)
:::

::: {.fragment style="margin-top: 1em;"}
**Applications:** Content delivery networks — Netflix, YouTube, Cloudflare
:::

---

[← Back to Topics](#toc)
